articles.title: RBA Will Be Better Prepared to Handle Next Crisis, Kent Says
articles.summary: Australian central bank Assistant Governor Chris Kent said the institution will be better prepared to respond to the next crisis it faces following a review of alternative monetary policy tools.

articles.title: US Stock Futures Rise, Oil Pares Climb on Iran: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: US equity-index futures climbed after reports the US and Iran backed away from a fresh escalation of their conflict, easing concerns over the fragile ceasefire underpinning peace talks.

articles.title: Oil Rises as US and Iran Set to Halt Attacks Following Flare-Up
articles.summary: Oil rose after a report that the US and Iran agreed to stop attacking each other, following a flare-up that saw a supertanker carrying Qatari crude hit near the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Sovereign Funds Pivot Further to Private Assets in Risky Markets
articles.summary: The world’s biggest public investors plan to shift more capital into private and less liquid assets amid rising risks to their traditional bond-and-stock portfolios, according to an industry survey.

articles.title: Wall Street Is Abandoning Bets on a Stronger Euro
articles.summary: Wall Street banks are capitulating on bets for a stronger euro, as markets see the US outpacing Europe on interest-rate hikes for the rest of this year.

articles.title: Prabowo Risks Prompt Global Banks to Pull Cash Out of Indonesia

articles.title: BlueBay Sees Near-Term Risk in Japan AI Stocks Followed by Rally
articles.summary: RBC BlueBay Asset Management remains bullish on Japanese AI-related stocks, expecting the rally to extend into 2027, while trimming near-term risk ahead of a potential slowdown in July and August.
